mission.title: Install and Configure Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP)
mission.description: Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) is a comprehensive enterprise Kubernetes platform that provides developers and IT operations teams with a powerful platform for building, deploying, and managing containerized applications. It is ideal for organizations looking for a robust solution with integrated developer tools and enterprise-grade security. Key trade-offs include the complexity of installation and management compared to simpler Kubernetes distributions.

steps.title: Install the OpenShift CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the OpenShift command-line interface (CLI) which is essential for interacting with your OpenShift cluster.

```bash

curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp/latest/openshift-client-linux-4.17.0.tar.gz

tar -xzf openshift-client-linux-4.17.0.tar.gz

sudo mv oc kubectl /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the OpenShift CLI for version 4.17.0.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the binaries to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create an OpenShift cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new OpenShift cluster with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

openshift-install create cluster --dir=./cluster --log-level=info

```

> Replace './cluster' with your desired directory for installation.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created OpenShift cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=$(pwd)/cluster/auth/kubeconfig

```

> Set the KUBECONFIG environment variable to point to your cluster's kubeconfig.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your OpenShift cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

oc get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-installation configuration
steps.description:
This step includes setting up monitoring, autoscaling, and RBAC for your OpenShift cluster.

```bash

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-monitoring-operator/release-4.17/deploy/cluster-monitoring-config.yaml

oc create clusterrolebinding cluster-admin-binding --clusterrole=cluster-admin --user=$(whoami)

```

> Apply the monitoring configuration.
> Create a cluster role binding for the current user.

uninstall.title: Remove the OpenShift cluster
uninstall.description:
This step will remove the OpenShift cluster and all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

openshift-install destroy cluster --dir=./cluster --log-level=info

```

> Ensure you have backups of any important data before proceeding.

uninstall.title: Clean up orphaned resources
uninstall.description:
This step removes any orphaned resources such as load balancers and persistent volumes.

```bash

aws ec2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[*].LoadBalancerArn' --output text | xargs -I {} aws elbv2 delete-load-balancer --load-balancer-arn {}

```

> Replace with your cloud provider's commands if not using AWS.
commands: aws ec2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[*].LoadBalancerArn' --output text | xargs -I {} aws elbv2 delete-load-balancer --load-balancer-arn {}

upgrade.title: Upgrade the OpenShift cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your cluster and its resources.

```bash

oc adm upgrade --to-image=<new-image>

oc get nodes

```

> Replace <new-image> with the desired version image.
> Verify that all nodes are upgraded and in 'Ready' state.

upgrade.title: Post-upgrade verification
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, verify that all components are functioning as expected.

```bash

oc get pods --all-namespaces

```

> Check that all pods are running without issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates an issue with the kubeconfig file or the cluster's API server certificate.

**Fix:**
Ensure your KUBECONFIG is set correctly and the cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no nodes available in cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster nodes are not in a 'Ready' state.

**Fix:**
Run 'oc get nodes' to check the status and troubleshoot any issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the image is not available in the specified registry.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and ensure it is accessible from the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up the cluster or adjust resource requests for your workloads.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for Kubernetes 1.30, enhanced security features, and improved developer experience tools.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy authentication methods and certain APIs.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Review the deprecation notices and update your configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with all nodes in 'Ready' state when running 'oc get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
